description: Fortinet FortiGate log type ID prefixes and severity levels

events.description: Forward traffic log - traffic passing through the firewall between interfaces. Primary traffic visibility source. Analyze for anomalous volumes, unusual destination ports, and geographic anomalies.
Local traffic log - traffic destined to/from the FortiGate itself. Monitor for management plane access attempts, unauthorized admin connections, and exploitation of FortiGate services.
Multicast traffic log - multicast routing events. Unusual multicast may indicate network reconnaissance or VXLAN-based lateral movement.
Sniffer traffic log - packet capture mode traffic. Indicates FortiGate in sniffer/one-arm mode. Useful for passive monitoring deployments.

System event - admin logins, config changes, firmware updates, HA events. Detect unauthorized management plane access, configuration tampering, and backdoor admin account usage. Critical for FortiGate compromise detection (CVE exploits targeting management).

VPN event - IPsec and SSL-VPN tunnel events. Monitor for VPN brute force (repeated failed authentications), unusual source countries, concurrent sessions, and tunnel establishment from unexpected IPs.
User event - user authentication events. Track logon/logoff, RADIUS/LDAP/TACACS+ auth. Monitor for credential stuffing and unauthorized access patterns.
Router event - dynamic routing protocol events (OSPF, BGP, RIP). Route hijacking or unexpected route changes may indicate network-level attacks.
Wireless event - WiFi events for FortiAP integration. Monitor for rogue AP detection, WPA attacks, and unauthorized wireless client connections.
WAD event - Web Application Detection proxy events. Application-level visibility through explicit/transparent proxy.
Endpoint event - FortiClient endpoint events. Endpoint compliance, malware detection, and vulnerability scan results from managed endpoints.
HA event - High Availability cluster events. Monitor for unexpected failovers which may indicate device tampering or DoS attacks against the active unit.
Compliance check event - security posture assessment results. Identify non-compliant endpoints and policy violations.
FortiExtender event - WAN extender events. Monitor for unauthorized cellular/WAN failover that may bypass network security controls.
Security rating event - FortiGate Security Fabric rating assessments. Track security posture changes over time.
FortiSDNConnector event - SDN integration events. Monitor for SDN policy changes and cloud connector health.
UTM virus/malware event - antivirus engine detections. Correlate source IP with endpoint inventory for infected host identification. Check if malware was blocked or only detected.

Web filter event - URL/category filtering decisions. Monitor for blocked categories being accessed (malware, phishing, newly registered domains). Users hitting blocked sites may indicate compromised endpoint or social engineering.
Application control event - application identification and enforcement. Detect shadow IT, unauthorized remote access tools, and policy violations.

IPS event - intrusion prevention signatures triggered. High/critical severity requires immediate triage. Correlate CVE with target asset vulnerability data. Repeated triggers from same source indicate active exploitation.
Anomaly event - protocol anomaly and rate-based detections. Detect DoS attacks, protocol violations, and traffic patterns outside normal baselines.
Email filter event - spam and phishing email detections. Monitor for targeted phishing campaigns and BEC attempts. Correlate with user click-through data.
DLP event - data loss prevention trigger. Investigate for data exfiltration attempts. Check matched pattern, destination, and volume. May indicate insider threat or compromised account.
VoIP event - voice/SIP protocol events. Monitor for toll fraud, VoIP scanning, and SIP-based attacks.
GTP event - GPRS Tunneling Protocol events (mobile network). Relevant for telecom environments, monitor for GTP-based attacks.
DNS event - DNS filtering and inspection results. Detect C2 domains, DGA patterns, DNS tunneling, and malicious domain lookups. Critical when combined with DNS Security license.
SSH filter event - SSH deep inspection events. Monitor for SSH tunneling abuse, brute force, and unauthorized SSH key usage.
SSL event - SSL/TLS inspection events. Certificate errors may indicate MITM. Monitor for connections using weak ciphers or expired certificates.
CIFS event - SMB/CIFS protocol events. Monitor for SMB-based lateral movement, ransomware file operations, and unauthorized file share access.
File filter event - file type-based filtering. Detect executable transfers, script file downloads, and file type masquerading.
ICAP event - Internet Content Adaptation Protocol events for external content scanning integration.
Virtual patch event - protection for known vulnerabilities without endpoint patching. Indicates vulnerability exploitation attempt against protected assets.
Severity 0 - system unusable. Immediate action required. Indicates critical hardware failure, firmware corruption, or system-level compromise.
Severity 1 - immediate action required. Critical security events requiring SOC response within minutes.
Severity 2 - critical condition. High-impact events that may affect security posture or service availability.
Severity 3 - error condition. Operational errors that may impact security monitoring or enforcement capabilities.
Severity 4 - warning condition. Conditions that may lead to errors or security gaps if not addressed.
Severity 5 - normal but significant. Standard operational events that confirm proper functioning.
Severity 6 - informational. Routine events useful for baseline and trending analysis.
Severity 7 - debug messages. Detailed diagnostic information, typically disabled in production.
